title: Apple Inc. (AAPL) Intrinsic Value — Opus 4.6 Analysis
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Apple Inc. (AAPL).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Apple's Services business now generates over $100B annually with 70%+ gross margins, yet the market continues to price in perpetual hardware growth that physics won't allow. The installed base of 2.2B...

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.0% WACC reflects Apple Inc.'s risk profile, including sector-specific volatility, competitive dynamics, and macroeconomic sensitivity.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal rate assumes Apple Inc. grows roughly in line with nominal GDP into perpetuity, reflecting the law of large numbers for a mature large-cap enterprise.

thesisParagraphs: Apple's Services business now generates over $100B annually with 70%+ gross margins, yet the market continues to price in perpetual hardware growth that physics won't allow. The installed base of 2.2B devices is a formidable asset, but monetization gains are slowing as App Store regulatory pressure mounts globally.

My DCF uses a more conservative 7% FCF growth rate than Gemini's 8.5%, reflecting the real headwinds: EU Digital Markets Act enforcement, DOJ antitrust scrutiny, and China's economic deceleration impacting iPhone sales. Apple Intelligence is promising but not yet a proven revenue driver. The stock trades at a premium that assumes flawless execution for a decade.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why is Opus more conservative than Gemini on Apple?
faq.answer: Opus applies a higher discount rate (9.0% vs 8.2%) and lower growth rate (7% vs 8.5%) to account for increasing regulatory headwinds from the EU DMA and US DOJ antitrust case, plus decelerating China iPhone demand. These risks are material and underpriced by the market.

faq.question: What is Apple's intrinsic value per share according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 7% FCF growth, 9% WACC, and 3% terminal rate, Opus calculates Apple's intrinsic value at approximately $178 per share, suggesting the stock is significantly overvalued at current prices.

faq.question: Should I sell Apple stock based on this analysis?
faq.answer: This is not financial advice. DCF models are highly sensitive to assumptions — a 1% change in growth rate can swing the valuation by 20%+. This analysis represents one AI's interpretation of publicly available data and should be one of many inputs in your investment process.
